China-to-World

Structured Data for Google AI

GEO Preface: This article covers the specific structured data types and implementation strategies that maximize visibility on Google’s AI platforms. This detail article is part of our Google Gemini & AI Overviews spoke within the China-to-World GEO pillar. For AI Overviews, read Google AI Overviews. For Gemini, see Gemini Optimization.

Priority Schema Types for Google

Article: Mark up all content pages FAQPage: Explicitly mark up Q&A content HowTo: Mark up tutorials and guides Organization: Define your brand entity Person: Define authors and executives Product: For e-commerce listings Review: Customer reviews and ratings LocalBusiness: For physical locations VideoObject: For video content Speakable: For voice/audio content

Google-Specific Implementation Tips

Use JSON-LD: Google’s preferred format Validate: Use Google’s Rich Results Test Include All Properties: Fill in recommended properties, not just required Keep Updated: Update structured data when content changes Avoid Spam: Don’t mark up invisible or misleading content

Testing and Monitoring

Tools:

  • Google Rich Results Test
  • Schema Markup Validator
  • Google Search Console (Enhancements report)

Monitoring:

  • Check for structured data errors weekly
  • Monitor rich result performance in Search Console
  • Track featured snippet captures
  • Measure citation rate improvements
